params.name: Papin Pot
params.description: The pressure cooker, the world's first, was invented by physicist Papin. He discovered that the boiling point of water changes with air pressure – the higher the pressure, the higher the boiling point. Therefore, he designed and created a sealed container to trap steam generated from heating water, creating a high-temperature, high-pressure environment that accelerated cooking efficiency.
